_meta.status: RESEARCH-GROUNDED DRAFT for clinical-lead sign-off (#58, 2026-06-25) — adopt, add, or cut any line
_meta.purpose: INTERIM mitigation for the keyword-detection fails-open finding (issue #65). Suppresses the #58 banned-opener rewrite (passes the reply through unchanged) when the turn looks sensitive but the keyword clinical-flag did not fire. Deliberately broader than CF-001..006 and allowed to over-match — a false match only costs a soft opener passing through, which Decision 2 established as the acceptable direction.
_meta.scope_guard: Gates the opener rewrite ONLY. Does NOT set clinical_flags, does NOT drive the 24h clinician-review escalation, does NOT affect crisis routing. The real fix for detection + escalation is the semantic tier in #65; this lexicon is not that fix and must not be mistaken for it.

research_basis.eating_concern: SCOFF (Sick/Control/One-stone/Fat/Food) + EDE-Q behavioural items: self-induced vomiting, restriction/fasting, laxative/compensatory exercise, bingeing, calorie/weight/body-image preoccupation.
research_basis.trauma_indicator: PCL-5 / PTSD disclosure language: sexual or physical victimisation phrasing, childhood-relational onset, intrusion symptoms (flashbacks, nightmares, reliving), non-disclosure ('never told anyone'). PFA principle: do not re-elicit the event.
research_basis.domestic_situation: HITS (Hurt/Insult/Threaten/Scream) + WAST + coercive-control framework: physical assault, coercive control (phone/finances/isolation/movement), and fear/threat language. Criterion 12 (device safety) applies downstream.
research_basis.substance_use: AUDIT + CAGE (Cut-down/Annoyed/Guilty/Eye-opener) + DAST: drinking/using to cope or numb, solitary/concealed use, tolerance/dependence and loss-of-control phrasing.
research_basis.psychotic_disclosure: Phenomenology of hallucinations (auditory/visual), persecutory delusions/paranoia, and thought-interference/delusions of reference. Clinical stance: validate distress without confirming or refuting content.

_meta.reviewer_note_ar: Arabic entries are Khaleeji/MSA best-effort and MOST need native-clinician review — translation fidelity is exactly where the bilingual gap lives.
_meta.match: case-insensitive substring; English phrases scanned against the user message (English) + the candidate reply; Arabic phrases scanned against the original (raw) message
